step_by_step_thinking:
The determination of the level of fluoride in community water fluoridation programmes does not directly correlate with mean annual temperatures, economic factors, average weights of children, or altitude. Instead, it focuses on maintaining an optimal balance to achieve caries prevention without causing harm through excess. Given current recommendations, the ideal concentration ranges from 0.7 to 1 ppm in many regions, adjusted locally if necessary considering existing supplementary fluoride sources.
